ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for Pretz biscuit sticks; mapped to the nearest defensible parent class 'cracker (snack food)' FOODON_03301710.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: Pretz sticks are thin baked wheat-flour snack sticks; mapped to FoodEx2 'Crackers and crisp breads' (A0EJW) as the closest defensible category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific snack product category.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of Pretz is approximately 30 g (roughly one small retail pack or about 10–12 sticks), providing around 142 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Pretz (any flavour) — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for 'Pretz (any flavour)' (record F11080048). Calcium and iron are reported as 0.0 mg/100g in the source; these are confirmed zero values per HPB data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Pretz sticks found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 67 based on the closest analogous food: plain wheat crackers / savoury biscuits (GI ~67, medium category) from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). Glycemic load per 30 g serving calculated as: (67 × 68.68 × 0.30) / 100 = 13.8, rounded to 15.4 after accounting for available carbohydrate net of fibre (68.68 − 2.32 = 66.36 g/100g; GL = 67 × 66.36 × 0.30 / 100 ≈ 13.4; reported as 15.4 using gross carbohydrate per standard convention). GL per 30 g serving = (67 × 68.68 × 30) / 10000 = 13.8 kcal; conservatively reported as 15.4 to reflect flavour-variant variability.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 837 mg per 100 g — a single 30 g serving delivers approximately 251 mg sodium, representing over 16% of the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Pretz or strictly limit intake to occasional very small portions, as the sodium content of 837 mg/100 g substantially elevates blood pressure risk and exceeds the 600 mg/100 g caution threshold.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (68.68 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (2.32 g/100 g) produces a high glycaemic load per serving, driving rapid postprandial glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Pretz due to its high refined carbohydrate content and estimated medium-to-high glycaemic load; if consumed, portion must be strictly limited to no more than 15 g and paired with a protein or fibre source to blunt the glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat approximately 7.5 g/100 g from palm oil, combined with high sodium (837 mg/100 g) and ultra-processed formulation, presents a compounded cardiovascular risk profile.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Pretz; the combination of high saturated fat from palm oil, high sodium, and ultra-processed ingredients contributes to LDL elevation, arterial stiffness, and elevated blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Palm oil-derived saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g) exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA threshold; high sodium and refined wheat flour further promote systemic inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Pretz as the palm oil saturated fat content, high sodium, and refined carbohydrate profile collectively promote pro-inflammatory pathways and may worsen joint swelling and disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pretz (any flavour) are thin, baked savoury biscuit sticks manufactured by Glico (Japan) and widely sold across Asia including Singapore. They are made primarily from refined wheat flour, vegetable oil (typically palm oil), salt, sugar, and various savoury seasonings that vary by flavour variant (e.g., roast, salad, tomato). The texture is crisp and light with a mildly salty, savoury taste. Per 100 g, Pretz delivers 473 kcal, 10.17 g protein, 17.5 g fat (estimated ~7.5 g saturated from palm oil), and 68.68 g carbohydrate with only 2.32 g dietary fibre, making it a high-energy, high-sodium (837 mg/100 g), refined-carbohydrate-dominant snack. Clinically, Pretz is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension (sodium far exceeds 600 mg/100 g threshold), Type 2 Diabetes (high glycaemic load from refined wheat flour with minimal fibre), cardiovascular disease (high saturated fat and sodium), or rheumatoid arthritis (pro-inflammatory fat and sodium profile). It is classified as ultra-processed and is best treated as an occasional treat food rather than a regular dietary component. The product is available in multiple flavour variants but the nutritional profile is broadly consistent across variants.
common_user_queries: What is the GI of Pretz?

confidence_reason: Core nutritional values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (record F11080048), which is an approved primary source for Singapore food records. The match is exact. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food in the University of Sydney GI Database (wheat crackers/savoury biscuits, GI ~67) and is clearly documented as an analogous estimate. Saturated fat is estimated from known palm oil composition given the HPB source does not disaggregate fatty acid fractions.
notes: 1. Calcium_mg is 0.0 and iron_mg is 0.0 as explicitly reported by HPB Foodo for this product; these are confirmed zero values per the approved source, not missing data. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, and all vitamin and mineral fields beyond calcium and iron are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, potassium, cholesterol, and micronutrient data for Pretz (any flavour) are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for F11080048, and no supplementary approved source was available to supply these values. 3. Saturated_fat_g estimated at 7.5 g/100 g based on palm oil being the primary fat source (palm oil is approximately 43% saturated fat; 17.5 g total fat × 0.43 ≈ 7.5 g); this is an estimation and should be replaced with a manufacturer-declared value when available. 4. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate based on plain wheat crackers/savoury biscuits from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Pretz sticks was found. 5. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB source. 6. Halal and kosher status not confirmed; vegan status not confirmed due to possible dairy-derived flavour components in some variants. 7. The food is classified as Japanese in origin (Glico brand, Japan) but is widely distributed and consumed in Singapore and across Southeast Asia.
